claim: Agent Kernel can connect to a live local model_stack server and complete a delegated queue job with provider=model_stack. This is a runtime/provider wiring proof, not a same-weights proof against the live vLLM Qwen model.
limitations: The queue job used a deterministic git_repo_review_direct command path, not a model_stack-generated JSON decision.
The tiny model-stack artifact proves local server/runtime connectivity, health, and token generation, not useful language-model decision quality.
The model artifact was a randomly initialized tiny Model Stack TransformerLM and was not the Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B weights served by the live vLLM server on 127.0.0.1:8000.
a5_interpretation: This closes the custom model_stack live server/model-dir wiring proof at the provider and delegated-queue level. It does not close same-weight model-stack inference for the current vLLM Qwen runtime. A stronger future proof would use a real instruction-tuned model_stack artifact that emits parseable Agent Kernel JSON decisions, ideally loaded from the same family of weights used by the production live-provider run.
